---
title: "[SOLVED] kubuntu 8.10 dual monitor problems"
date: 2008-11-15
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by anomalous_underdog on 2008-11-15
my second monitor is set to run a separate x screen, but it doesn't load up anything at startup. is there no way to add commands so at least a window manager gets run on that second x screen? I've put commands in my .xinitrc but it doesn't get called at all.

my xorg.conf

my .xinitrc
```

xsetwacom set pad Screen_No "-1"
xsetwacom set stylus Screen_No "0"
xsetwacom set eraser Screen_No "0"
xsetwacom set cursor Screen_No "-1"
xsetwacom set stylus BottomY "9425"
xsetwacom set stylus BottomX "2448"
xsetwacom set stylus TopY "-200"
xsetwacom set stylus TopX "439"
xsetwacom set stylus TPCButton "off"
xsetwacom set stylus mode "Absolute"
xsetwacom set stylus Button3 "Button 3"
xsetwacom set stylus Button2 "Button 2"
xsetwacom set stylus Button1 "Button 1"
xsetwacom set cursor Accel "1"
xsetwacom set cursor SpeedLevel "7"
xsetwacom set stylus Suppress "2"
xsetwacom set stylus RawSample "4"
xsetwacom set stylus ClickForce "5"
xsetwacom set stylus PressCurve "0 50 50 100"
xsetwacom set eraser Suppress "2"
xsetwacom set eraser RawSample "4"
xsetwacom set eraser ClickForce "5"
xsetwacom set eraser PressCurve "0 50 50 100"
# run the primary system script
. /etc/X11/xinit/xinitrc

gkrellm &

DISPLAY=:0.1 openbox
exec openbox-session

```

---

### Post by anomalous_underdog on 2008-11-22
I added "DISPLAY=:0.1 openbox &" to my openbox's autostart.sh. It did the trick.

---
